source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/250116 ★인도(CDSCO)(재) MC, 23G/241230 - 인도 MC 제조소등록/Risk management/ARMR-FMEA-0-001 Risk Table 19.9.17.pdf

document.title: ARMR-FMEA-0-001 Risk Table
document.documentType: Risk management risk table / residual risk evaluation

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management table evaluating IEC 60601-1 clauses, hazards, controls, and residual risk acceptability for ME equipment.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management file evidence by identifying hazards, estimating risks, documenting risk controls, and recording residual risk acceptability.
keyPoints: Document file number is ARMR-FMEA-0-001 with Rev. No. 0 dated SEP/17/2019.
Residual risk evaluation references IEC 60601-1 clauses and ISO 14971 risk management activities.
Essential performance includes acquiring an image within 5 seconds and avoiding two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display in acquired images.
Multiple initial risks are rated unacceptable and controlled through design, inspection standards, labeling, user manuals, work instructions, test reports, and software validation.
Revision history records Rev. 0 on SEP/17/2019 as Establishment.

concepts.name: Essential Performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance. - Must be able to acquire image within 5 seconds

concepts.name: Risk management process
concepts.evidence: Has the risk management process been applied in accordance with ISO 14971 to select the tests to be performed?

concepts.name: Single fault condition
concepts.evidence: The single fault condition described in 13.2 applies.

concepts.name: Leakage current
concepts.evidence: RM-02 leakage current Can deliver unacceptable current to the patient or operator in a single fault condition

concepts.name: Safety signs
concepts.evidence: Q14 7.5 Safety signs A ISO 3864-1: 2002 Cover corresponding to Clause 7 and ISO 7010

concepts.name: Software validation
concepts.evidence: software for centrifuge and control is applied: software validation report

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility
concepts.evidence: 17 Electromagnetic compatibility of ME equipment and ME systems

evidence.evidenceText: Has the risk management process been applied in accordance with ISO 14971 to select the tests to be performed?

evidence.claim: Essential performance includes image acquisition within 5 seconds.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance. - Must be able to acquire image within 5 seconds

evidence.claim: The acquired image should not have two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display.
evidence.evidenceText: The acquired image should not have two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display.

evidence.claim: For single fault condition leakage current risk, approved AC-DC transformer, AC cable, and fuse are specified as controls.
evidence.evidenceText: Designed to use approved AC-DC transformer, AC cable, fuse to prevent leakage current

evidence.claim: The product has no patient-contact part.
evidence.evidenceText: This product do not have any comtact to patient part.

evidence.claim: The product does not use lithium batteries or a fuel cell.
evidence.evidenceText: This product do not use lithium batteries or fuel cell.

evidence.claim: FUSE and AC power cables are user replaceable.
evidence.evidenceText: FUSE and AC power cables are user replaceable

evidence.claim: Software validation reports are used as risk control evidence for programmable electrical medical system clauses.
evidence.evidenceText: software for centrifuge and control is applied: software validation report

evidence.claim: AC power cable, data cable, LAN cable, and trigger cable can be removed without tools.
evidence.evidenceText: AC power cable, data cable, LAN cable, trigger cable can be removed without tools

evidence.claim: Electromagnetic compatibility risk is addressed because the product is used in general practice rooms and may affect ME devices in the medical room.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is used in general practice rooms and it can affect electromagnetic effects on various ME devices in the medical room.

evidence.claim: An EMC Test report is listed as implementation evidence for electromagnetic waves risk.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-19 electromagnetic waves ... EMC Test report

reviewFlags.reason: Product model or model name is not present in the extracted source text.
Authority or jurisdiction is not stated in the source text; source path suggests an India/CDSCO filing context but the document content does not identify an authority.
OCR/text quality is low in places, including misspellings such as 'comtact', 'Mraking', 'faliures', 'harzadous', and malformed standard text such as 'ISO 7010: 20030W001'.
Several applicability conclusions are written in non-native English and may require source PDF review for exact official wording.
